scoring.strong: IC50 < 100nM or Kd < 100nM with multiple assays

target_brca_relevance.description: Target relevance to breast cancer biology

scoring.strong: Target overexpressed in BRCA + driver mutation/pathway

clinical_evidence.description: Clinical trial or real-world evidence in cancer/BRCA

scoring.strong: Phase 3+ trial in BRCA or approved for BRCA

mechanism_rationale.description: Mechanism-based rationale for repurposing to BRCA

scoring.strong: Clear mechanistic hypothesis with supporting data
High ADMET score (>50), known safety profile
scoring.weak: Low ADMET (<10) or black box warning

drug_target_evidence.evidence: Potent DOT1L inhibitor (IC50 ~0.3nM). Crystal structure available. Multiple publications.

target_brca_relevance.evidence: DOT1L involved in DNA repair and MLL-rearranged leukemia. Limited direct BRCA evidence.
target_brca_relevance.pathway: Chromatin histone methylation - relevant to DNA damage response
target_brca_relevance.notes: Epigenetic regulation may affect BRCA tumor growth

clinical_evidence.evidence: Preclinical only. Trials in MLL-rearranged leukemia, not BRCA.

mechanism_rationale.evidence: Epigenetic modulation + DNA damage response. Potential synergy with DNA damaging agents.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: DOT1L inhibition may sensitize BRCA tumors to PARP inhibitors

safety_profile.evidence: Highest ADMET score (119.12). No major toxicity flags.

next_steps: Test in BRCA cell lines (GDSC validation confirms sensitivity)
Combination studies with PARP inhibitors or chemo

drug_target_evidence.evidence: FDA-approved BTK inhibitor. IC50 ~0.5nM. Extensive bioactivity data.
drug_target_evidence.chembl_status: Approved drug with extensive target validation

target_brca_relevance.evidence: BTK primarily in B-cell signaling. Limited direct BRCA relevance.
target_brca_relevance.pathway: B-cell receptor signaling - not typical BRCA pathway

clinical_evidence.evidence: Approved for CLL, MCL. Some trials in solid tumors.
clinical_evidence.notes: Limited efficacy in solid tumors as monotherapy

mechanism_rationale.evidence: GDSC predicts sensitivity, but mechanism unclear. May affect immune microenvironment.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: BTK inhibition could modulate immune cells in tumor microenvironment

safety_profile.evidence: High ADMET score (111.90). Known safety profile from CLL use.
safety_profile.notes: Bleeding risk, atrial fibrillation in some patients

drug_validations.recommendation: EXPLORATORY - Mechanism unclear, but approved drug with good safety

drug_target_evidence.chembl_status: Approved drug with extensive bioactivity data

target_brca_relevance.evidence: HER2 overexpressed in 15-20% of breast cancers. EGFR relevant in TNBC.
target_brca_relevance.notes: Known BRCA drug, validates prediction model

clinical_evidence.evidence: FDA-approved for HER2+ BRCA. Multiple Phase 3 trials.
clinical_evidence.notes: Standard of care for HER2+ BRCA (with capecitabine)

mechanism_rationale.evidence: Inhibits HER2-driven proliferation and survival. Well-established mechanism.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: Dual EGFR/HER2 blockade in HER2+ or EGFR-driven tumors

safety_profile.evidence: High ADMET score (98.68). Well-known safety profile.

drug_validations.recommendation: POSITIVE CONTROL - Validates prediction model accuracy
next_steps: Consider novel combinations with predicted novel drugs

target_brca_relevance.evidence: PARP inhibition = synthetic lethal with BRCA1/2 mutations.
target_brca_relevance.pathway: Genome integrity - validated in BRCA1/2-mutant tumors
target_brca_relevance.notes: Approved for germline BRCA-mutated BRCA/ovarian cancer

clinical_evidence.evidence: FDA-approved for BRCA1/2-mutated breast/ovarian cancer. Multiple Phase 3 trials.
clinical_evidence.notes: Standard of care for BRCA-mutated tumors

mechanism_rationale.evidence: Synthetic lethality with HR-deficiency. Well-established mechanism.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: PARP trapping prevents DNA repair in HR-deficient cells

safety_profile.evidence: Moderate ADMET score (24.12). Known toxicity profile.
safety_profile.notes: Risk of AML/MDS with prolonged use

drug_validations.recommendation: POSITIVE CONTROL - Validates prediction model for genomic subtypes
next_steps: Model validation confirmed for BRCA-mutated subset

drug_target_evidence.evidence: Potent ATR inhibitor. IC50 ~1nM. In clinical trials.
drug_target_evidence.chembl_status: Clinical candidate with strong bioactivity data

target_brca_relevance.evidence: ATR essential for replication stress response. Synthetic lethal with ATM loss.
target_brca_relevance.pathway: Genome integrity - relevant to DNA damage response in BRCA
target_brca_relevance.notes: ATR inhibition sensitizes to DNA damage in HR-deficient tumors

clinical_evidence.evidence: Multiple Phase 1/2 trials in solid tumors including BRCA.

mechanism_rationale.evidence: ATR inhibition synergizes with replication stress and PARP inhibitors.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: Combines with PARP inhibitors in HR-deficient BRCA tumors
mechanism_rationale.notes: Strong mechanistic rationale - clinical validation ongoing

safety_profile.evidence: Low ADMET score (9.52). Manageable toxicity in trials.
safety_profile.notes: Combination with DNA damaging agents increases toxicity

drug_validations.recommendation: STRONG CANDIDATE - Clinical validation ongoing, excellent mechanistic rationale
next_steps: Optimize dosing in combination with PARP inhibitors
Identify predictive biomarkers (ATM status, replication stress)

drug_target_evidence.evidence: FDA-approved DNA-damaging agent. Induces dsDNA breaks.

target_brca_relevance.evidence: DNA damage relevant to BRCA, but not selective.
target_brca_relevance.notes: May be more effective in HR-deficient tumors

clinical_evidence.evidence: Approved for testicular cancer, Hodgkin lymphoma. Limited use in BRCA.
clinical_evidence.trials: Historical use, not current standard for BRCA
clinical_evidence.notes: Replaced by newer agents due to toxicity

mechanism_rationale.evidence: DNA damage should be effective in proliferating tumors.
mechanism_rationale.hypothesis: dsDNA breaks overwhelm repair in BRCA tumors

safety_profile.evidence: Low ADMET score (7.83). Pulmonary fibrosis risk.
safety_profile.admet: Black box warning for pulmonary toxicity
safety_profile.notes: Dose-limiting pulmonary fibrosis - limits clinical use

drug_validations.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED - Toxicity outweighs benefits, replaced by newer agents
next_steps: Do not pursue for BRCA repurposing

drug_target_evidence.chembl_status: Approved drug with extensive bioactivity data

target_brca_relevance.evidence: Mitotic inhibition effective in rapidly dividing BRCA tumors.

clinical_evidence.evidence: FDA-approved for BRCA. Standard neoadjuvant/metastatic regimen.
clinical_evidence.notes: Standard of care in neoadjuvant and metastatic settings

mechanism_rationale.evidence: Mitotic catastrophe in proliferating cells. Well-established.

safety_profile.evidence: Low ADMET score (7.45). Known toxicity (neutropenia, neuropathy).
safety_profile.notes: Requires growth factor support, dose-limiting toxicity

drug_validations.recommendation: POSITIVE CONTROL - Validates prediction model for chemotherapy
next_steps: Focus on identifying novel drugs to reduce chemo burden
